mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138495.0 thousand as of June 2013 reflects ongoing post-recession employment recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% in a tightening cycle signals monetary constraint that reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and increased borrowing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.24% as of June 2013 indicates elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS accelerates above 2% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer spending momentum that translates to higher corporate revenues and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382928.0 million as of June 2013 reflects consumer demand strength
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with RSAFS growth > 2% produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declines month-over-month while unemployment rate UNRATE rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9019 and UNRATE at 5.0% as of May-June 2013
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with simultaneous supply contraction and labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (INDPRO decline AND UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST fall below 1,000 thousand units annualized, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1187.0 thousand as of May 2013, above threshold but vulnerable to decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months when HOUST < 1000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, signaling monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression and equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real yields decline.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565 and FEDFUNDS at 4.24% as of May-June 2013
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with inflation acceleration and policy lag
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate UNRATE declines below 5.0% month-over-month, signaling tightening labor market conditions that increase wage pressure and corporate labor costs, reducing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of June 2013, at threshold level
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months when UNRATE < 5.0% and declining, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales RSAFS grow month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand expansion across consumption and output that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11658.9 billion (Q1 2013) and RSAFS at 382928.0 million (June 2013)
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with broad-based growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
